# Producer archive goldens

These binary fixtures are generated by the real Toolkit and Studio archive
writers. They are committed so each consumer can test the other producer
without importing its runtime implementation.

Update them deliberately from the workspace root:

```powershell
cd PkuHoleToolkit
npm run fixture:archive-contract

cd ..\PkuHoleStudio
$env:PKUHOLE_ARCHIVE_FIXTURE_OUTPUT = "..\PkuHoleArchiveSpec\fixtures\v2\archives\valid\studio-media.treehole.zip"
go test ./internal/archive -run TestUpdateArchiveContractGolden -count=1

cd ..\PkuHoleArchiveSpec
npm run vendor:workspace
npm run release:workspace
```

Contract 2.1 goldens use ZIP `STORE`. Readers may support more compression
methods, but interoperability tests must always cover this baseline.
